mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate sits at an extremely low level (0.12%), reflecting accommodative monetary policy that compresses the equity risk premium and lowers the discount rate applied to future cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of low FEDFUNDS show forward returns not significantly different from baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days, with the effect strongest late in an economic expansion.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC conditional on trigger remains below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deceleration of industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) over a trailing 3-month window signals a synchronized supply-demand contraction that individually neither series reliably predicts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days only when both series confirm weakness simultaneously.
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances show forward 60d returns statistically indistinguishable from single-series trigger instances (p > 0.05), indicating no incremental interaction effect beyond individual series.

mechanism_narrative: A decline of more than 5% in housing starts (HOUST) over 3 months predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days only when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2%, because rate-sensitive housing weakness amplifies broader economic slowdown fears during monetary tightening; the same HOUST decline carries no significant signal when policy rates are near zero.
failure_condition: Triggered instances during tightening regimes show forward 20d returns not significantly more negative than non-triggered instances (p > 0.05), or the effect appears equally strong when FEDFUNDS is near zero.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) turning negative month-over-month for two consecutive months signals an abrupt labor market inflection that triggers rapid risk-off repositioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS m/m change < 0 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX exhibits positive or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC conditional on trigger stays below 0.03 across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained disinflation trend in CPIAUCSL, reflected in declining year-over-year inflation, lowers expected future discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between CPIAUCSL year-over-year trend and forward 60d SPX return remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth accelerating above 2.5% annualized quarter-over-quarter signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days during expansion phases of the business cycle.
failure_condition: Triggered instances of GDPC1 acceleration show forward 60d returns not significantly higher than baseline unconditional returns (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects real economic momentum that translates into short-horizon equity strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO m/m growth and forward 5d SPX return remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or sign concordance rate falls below 52%.
